reviews.review_msg: Open-book quizzes with asynchronous lectures. Really organized with Canvas and email. Content can be tough to grasp but Betty teaches us to think critically. I recommend taking her class after youve taken a few SFU English courses beforehand. As the reviewer below said, she will not hold your hand but she will do her best to help you succeed.

The assignments were pretty straightforward and Betty offers examples of what she wants. She also gave us flexibility with our evaluations which was nice. She is really helpful in office hours and answers emails really fast. I really enjoyed this class and I would recommend taking it if you actually want to learn how to read and write.

Betty is very knowledgeable and well respected, but she seems to have difficulty translating this into engaging lectures, and she is an incredibly nit-picky and particular marker. Also, she is not very understanding of students' extenuating circumstances as she refuses to budge on due dates (even in the middle of a pandemic).

Betty is a very kind, respected prof and I quite enjoy our synchronous lectures (taking it online). That being said, she gives difficult assignments with very little instructions and grades tough. I would recommend her but be prepared to work really hard on all your papers for only an okay grade.

Betty truly cares about teaching her students. She puts time and thought into every class. That being said, she expects you to stay on top of the readings and engage with the course content--but her passion for her field is contagious. Betty's course was more than worth my time and money and she is a quality educator. Highly recommended!

If you like to be bored, take this class. The MOST useless topics are included. Her lectures are full of nonsense and random things that fall out of topic. I understand english may be mundane BUT DO NOT take a class with her if you hate being off topic all the time. Example: 50 min lecture, 10 mins of useful notes

I would say your marks are all depended on your TA. Here I just want to address Maya,my best TA this term, always provides help and is super nice. Betty lectures well, but her stressful 10 minutes in class writings are not fair. Especially her super tough marking scheme.

Your mark depends on your TA, lectures are just good to go to for in class writings worth marks. Had Rawia as my TA & she was absolutely amazing I couldn't believe it was her first time TA'ing! She had great office hours, was very helpful, genuinely cared, and was a fair marker. She prepared us well for each essay & is super approachable !

90% of lectures were useless, I showed up only bc there are random in class writings for marks which is ridiculous bc it only tests you ability to write fast and under pressure in less than 10 minutes?? Your mark is based off 2 essays, midterm (worth 5%), final, tutorial mark, and than small things like preparation etc worth 10%.
Very enthusiastic about content, and a lot of grading depends on lecture material.

I get more and more bored each lecture not gonna lie but it's a fairly easy class other than the two essays you have to write but it's a W course so that's the norm. I debate on skipping lectures but you go over some interesting things sometimes in relation to your readings and sometimes you'll have an in lecture assignment to do. Midterm was easy

Amazing teacher. Readings were always interesting and informative, presenting a wide view of the world rather than zeroing in on the straight white male perspective. Lectures were very interesting and I learned a lot. Her class was the highlight of my semester.

Lots of readings and difficult to complete since you only had two days to complete it. i found her syllabus to be rather borring she is very informative and easily accessible outside of class. Overall would not take again as it lowered my GPA significantly

Gives a very heavy reading syllabus, and is a very tough marker. She's highly respected with an international research pull, and this shows. Not the most approachable prof, but kind once you get to know her. Only take her if you're willing to work, because if you succeed she'll recognize that.

Betty does not use Canvas or any technology, it's all done on a projector during lectures. Her midterms and exams are more history-based than literary based. A lot of her assignments are fit for 3rd or 4th years, not 2nd year classes.

I took this class because nothing else fit into my schedule. Overall I found the class quite boring and the lectures droned on. Following Betty's lecture notes was confusing because she was all over the place. I also found her intimidating and judgemental which caused me to refrain from participating in class.

SHE IS TOUGH. SHE WILL NOT HOLD YOUR HAND. BUT YOU WILL LEARN A LOT IF YOU PAY ATTENTION. She IS incredibly helpful and caring. Speak to anyone in the department, she's the most respected one (nationally/globally) out of all of them and she could go to any university she wanted to. Take her class if you actually care about the craft.

She is a nice lady. Her office hour is quite useful if you have any questions with essays. She will give you useful suggestions on essays. The midterm is not that hard, but the final exam and the second essay are difficult. Get ready to read.

I really enjoyed this course. Betty chose a wide range of readings that kept lectures interesting. The grading criteria is clear, and the Midterm and Final are fair (based on materials talked about in lecture). Paper topics were interesting to write about. Overall, if you do the readings, and attend lecture/tutorial, you will do just fine.
She's such a sweet professor (if you go talk to her in office hours).. but.. sigh.  Readings/quizzes aren't great. Welp, I hope I do well on the final.  Read the comment below.

This class is incredibly hard and complex. Betty is a very sweet lady but she expects way TOO much from her undergraduate students. Having a night 4 hours lecture has never been easy but this one exceeds boredom. She is a very though grader. Also, the class is supposed to be about the 18th century authors and we never discussed about them. Awful.

Betty ran a difficult first year English course. There were many short readings, and lectures were confusing at times. She points out kids at random to answer questions in class, so sit close to the back if you are not confident with the material. Tests, quizzes and papers were marked fairly, but you must put in the time to achieve a high grade.

She and her TAs are tough graders. For a first year English course, she assigns a lot of readings and expects way too much from her students. She does not give out proper notes and her explanations are confusing. As a lecturer she is dull, awkward and lacks passion. However, she does provide good feedback on writing assignments.
Betty is a good prof. Her curriculum can be sort of inflexible, but she is a fair marker and gives really useful feedback. You can tell she cares about the students.

The four hour class on 18th Century Literature consisted of short quizzes and writing assignments that had nothing to do with the readings.  Her "mini-lectures" of authors and writings might very well be from wikipedia and she is not open to discussion but prefers to have students echo her own beliefs. Waste. Of. Time.

She is way too hard on her students, expects too much, and there is too much reading. In addition to the long novels, there are long articles which, for the most part, have nothing to do with the focus of this course. She focuses a lot on city versus country. Will never take another course with her again!

an okay prof. seems nice and personable but her focus is all over the place and she is unorganized. the 5 novels were too much to read, and 3 of them seemed very repetetive. some articles we read were obscure and relating them to the material was a stretch. you won't get out of this class what you are required to put in, take with someone else.

Betty is an AMAZING prof. Yes, she is hard - I had to work my ass off for a decent mark. But, her comments and feedback are incredibly helpful - if you pay attention to what she's telling you, you WILL become a better writer. Lots of reading, yes. City/Country thing? I liked it - not so much a "hidden agenda" as a focal point/theme.

She seems to have a hidden agenda. Her focus for our class is more on the "city-country" theme than on the actual texts (possibly the focus of her own research?). Reading amount is too high even for a lit class. Very, very boring. Classmates complained of marking. On the first paper, the highest mark was an A-. And the class is very intelligent.
IF you take her 320 course be warned that most of her focus is on poetry and women poets. If you dislike poetry as much as I do, do not take this course with her.
She's very organized and knows how to teach well. She reminds me of a younger version of my grandma. She's very sweet and understanding.

Lots of material, expects a TON of background historical knowledge before start.  Midterm was *hard*, tested historical dates almost more than lit. Good intentions, but intimidating. Lots of 4th yrs took it so prof & my TA assumed everyone was 4th yr.Awesome lecturer. Wouldn't reccomend S for a Survey class. (not my TA, maybe better if she is TA?)

Nice, knowledgable, willing to listen.  Midterm was difficult-requires close analysis and the history part was  a surprise of historical stuff that was new to the class.  Like most English major people, she is finicky.
Awful... history lessons with no explanations of the work. For an intro course she expects a much higher understanding of the work than most students had. Boring. Nice lady though.
Worst English prof i've had. Do not take her if you have any ideas of your own. She only like students who spew back the crap she says in lecture.

She has a high interest in the course material but everyone is right- she seems more into relaying the history of the period than getting into the actual works. The midterm was...Bettyesque and more than half the class misunderstood the essay q resulting in low marks.

She seems like a nice lady, and she's not a bad prof - she's just not great. The reading list was okay, the midterm was fair, but the essay topics are a bit vague. I sometimes felt as though I was in a history class instead of an english class.

She's all over the place and spends most of her lecture time on material she didn't assign for reading. She really focuses on the history and doesn't really cover the actual readings that are required. Her tests are really off too, with obscure, vague essay topics. To top it off, she's boring in lecture.
No Comments

Material is boring, and the TA's were of no help and marked unjust. She rambles on, never lets you leave on time for ur next class. Disorganized notes and time preparation. I learnt everything in this course on my own and did well.
Found her kind of annoying.  I got an A though so I'm not complaining.

She is really nice, approachable and understanding... very willing to help and take suggestions from her students.  She expects good work and prep and encourages discussion.  My only complaint is that sometimes discussions were too narrowed, like she had a certain answer in mind and didn't always allow other discussions/theories fully develop.

I enjoyed Schellenberg's lectures; she's comprehensive, open to discussion, but has a definite ground to stand on.  However, the subject material was often appalling, as the class was analysis for context, not content.  There were 6 or 7 good works, all in the first quarter of the class or so.
She is the best professor I have ever had.  Thank goodness for her!  She has inspired me to become an English major and anyone who wants to do a lot of work to get a good mark it all pays off!
She is very confusing and the TA's don't help much.  She rambles and a lot of reading.
No Comments
HARD!
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

VERY high standards, tough marker. Does focus alot on history. But, in the end i got a good mark because i approached her for essay ideas and she gave alot of help. She is nice if she knows your working your ass off.
A very traditional teacher.  Straight forward, lots of relevant history with the literature.  Fair marker, but wants preparation and decent work.
worst class i ever took. more boring than a stats class.
205 talks about hell enough without having to make it a hermeneutic experience.  sympathy for the devil is one thing, betty schellenberg another.
wacked out teacher. talks about history more than english. sheesh!  not very nice either.
not too exciting but very straightforward..good
Knowledgeable, open to new ideas.
I didn't even study for midterm and got B--classmates were lazy and annoying
midterm so difficult that ta's had trouble with it
